_callStabilityFeeTracking(uint256,addres.details: This is called via try/catch to maintain compatibility with standard UBIFeeSplitter.

closeVault(bytes32).details: `drip()` settles fees before debt is zeroed; ordering is required by the chi      accumulator. `nonReentrant` blocks cross-function reentry via the gusd/collateral      tokens.

params.amount: Token amount to deposit (in collateral token decimals)

drip(bytes32).details: External mint/approve/splitFee calls happen against trusted gusd + feeSplitter      contracts. Accumulator state updates inside this function are CEI-correct      (chi/lastDrip update before external mint+splitFee), but Slither flags      `ilkDebt`/`acc.chi` writes that occur in CALLERS after `drip()` returns.      That is the canonical MakerDAO pattern: drip settles the fee accumulator BEFORE      the caller mutates `totalNormalizedDebt`. Reordering would charge fees on debt      that does not yet exist. All callers use `nonReentrant`.

liquidate(bytes32,address).details: `drip()` settles chi before we mark the vault as liquidated; ordering is required      so liquidation uses the up-to-date debt. `nonReentrant` blocks cross-function reentry      via the gusd/collateral tokens and the StabilityPool offset hook.

mintGUSD(bytes32,uint256).details: MakerDAO-style accumulator: `drip()` must settle the stability-fee chi BEFORE the new      mint changes `totalNormalizedDebt`; otherwise the fee would be charged against the new      debt as if it had been outstanding the whole time. `nonReentrant` blocks the cross-      function reentry path Slither warns about.

repayGUSD(bytes32,uint256).details: `drip()` must run first so chi reflects accrued fees BEFORE we reduce      `totalNormalizedDebt`; reordering would leak fees on the repaid portion.      `nonReentrant` blocks cross-function reentry.

_callStabilityFeeTracking(uint256,addres.notice: Helper function to call enhanced stability fee tracking.         Attempts to use StableUBIFeeSplitter.splitStabilityFee() if available.

acceptAdmin().notice: Step 2 of the two-step admin transfer.         Must be called by the previously proposed admin. Reverts otherwise.

depositCollateral(bytes32,uint256).notice: Deposit collateral into caller's vault for `ilk`.

healthFactor(bytes32,address).notice: Health factor of a vault (WAD-scaled). >= 1e18 is healthy.

ilkDebt(bytes32).notice: ilk -> total actual debt outstanding (in gUSD)

openVault(bytes32).notice: Open a vault for `ilk`. Vaults are implicitly created on first deposit,         but this function emits an event and validates the ilk exists.

pendingAdmin().notice: Pending admin for the two-step admin transfer (GOO-493).

pendingFee(bytes32).notice: Preview accrued (but not yet dripped) stability fee for an ilk.

transferAdmin(address).notice: Step 1 of the two-step admin transfer (GOO-493).         The current admin proposes a new admin; ownership remains with         the existing admin until the proposed admin calls `acceptAdmin`.

vaultDebt(bytes32,address).notice: Current actual debt for a vault (includes accrued fees).

withdrawCollateral(bytes32,uint256).notice: Withdraw collateral from caller's vault.         Vault must remain healthy after withdrawal.

         Must be called by the previously proposed admin. Reverts otherwise.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes32) view external returns (struct ICollateralRegistry.CollateralConfig memory)

documentation.text:
@dev External mint/approve/splitFee calls happen against trusted gusd + feeSplitter
      contracts. Accumulator state updates inside this function are CEI-correct
      (chi/lastDrip update before external mint+splitFee), but Slither flags
      `ilkDebt`/`acc.chi` writes that occur in CALLERS after `drip()` returns.
      That is the canonical MakerDAO pattern: drip settles the fee accumulator BEFORE
      the caller mutates `totalNormalizedDebt`. Reordering would charge fees on debt
      that does not yet exist. All callers use `nonReentrant`.

typeDescriptions.typeString: function (bytes32) view external returns (struct ICollateralRegistry.CollateralConfig memory)

documentation.text:
@notice Open a vault for `ilk`. Vaults are implicitly created on first deposit,
         but this function emits an event and validates the ilk exists.

documentation.text:
@dev MakerDAO-style accumulator: `drip()` must settle the stability-fee chi BEFORE the new
      mint changes `totalNormalizedDebt`; otherwise the fee would be charged against the new
      debt as if it had been outstanding the whole time. `nonReentrant` blocks the cross-
      function reentry path Slither warns about.

documentation.text:
@notice Returns true if vault health factor >= 1.0 (healthy).
   healthFactor = collateralValue * WAD / (actualDebt * liquidationRatio)

@dev RAY-precision exponentiation (binary exponentiation).
      result = base^n with base and result in RAY units.
      Originally implemented with inline assembly for gas efficiency.
      Rewritten in pure Solidity so that forge coverage (which disables
      the optimizer) can compile it — the assembly version exceeds the
      EVM stack limit after coverage instrumentation adds extra slots.
